title: rosa canina subvar. eriostyla
intro: Rosa canina subvar. eriostyla, also known as the Wild Rose of the Caucasus, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina plant, a flowering shrub native to the mountainous regions of the Caucasus. The plant's medicinal properties lie in its high concentration of vitamins, flavonoids, and essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rosa canina subvar. eriostyla include reducing inflammation and improving skin health, as well as supporting immune function and cardiovascular health. Rosa canina subvar. eriostyla can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or dried flower powder, which can be used to create skincare products and remedies.
